preamble: The shift from [ε] to [y] is a complex process of rounding and raising, typically observed in historical chain shifts where mid-vowels move toward the close-front space.

examples.note: Greek iotacism: diphthong οι monophthongized first to [ø] (open-mid front rounded) then to [y] (close front rounded) in Hellenistic/Byzantine Greek, before ultimately merging with [i] in Modern Greek.
